title: Side-by-side diff viewer for comparing current and draft content
purpose: Displays a visual comparison between current and draft content with color-coded additions and deletions. Used to help users understand changes in topic content.
module_context: Exports a DraftDiffViewer component that accepts current and draft content as props, along with optional language and class name. Includes helper functions for computing diffs and determining styling classes.

methods.name: computeDiff
methods.normalized_params: (current: string, draft: string): { currentLines: DiffLine[]; draftLines: DiffLine[] }

steps.content: Split the current content into lines.
Split the draft content into lines.
Create sets of trimmed lines for both current and draft content for efficient lookup.
Process each line of current content to determine if it was removed or unchanged.
Process each line of draft content to determine if it was added or unchanged.
Return the computed lines for both current and draft content with their statuses.
Return a background color class based on the line status.
Use green for added lines.
Use red for removed lines.
Use yellow for modified lines.
Return empty string for unchanged lines.
Return a text color class based on the line status.
Use green for added lines.
Use red for removed lines.
Use yellow for modified lines.
Use gray for unchanged lines.
Return a line number color class based on the line status.
Use green for added lines.
Use red for removed lines.
Use gray for unchanged lines.
Render a panel header with the provided title.
Render panel content area with scrollable overflow.
IF the lines array is empty or contains only an empty line, display the empty message.
ELSE render each line with its corresponding background and text colors.
Display line numbers with appropriate styling.
Compute the diff between current and draft content using the computeDiff function.
Calculate statistics for additions and deletions from the computed diff.
Render a stats bar showing language and addition/deletion counts.
Render the current content panel on the left side.
Render a divider between the panels.
Render the draft content panel on the right side.
